  "text": "Firefox's CSS calc() implementation spans both Rust (Servo) and C++ layers.\n\n## Parsing (1): CSS calc() Expression Parsing\n\nThe primary parsing code lives in the Rust Servo style engine:\n\n- **`servo/components/style/values/specified/calc.rs`**: Defines the `MathFunction` enum (calc, min, max, clamp, round, mod, rem, sin, cos, tan, etc.), the `Leaf` enum for leaf node types (Length, Angle, Time, Resolution, Percentage, Number), and the `CalcLengthPercentage` struct. It also defines `AllowParse` which controls what units and additional functions (like anchor()) are permitted when parsing.\n\n- **`servo/components/style/values/generics/calc.rs`**: Defines `GenericCalcNode<L>` (the calc expression AST with Sum, Product, MinMax, Clamp, Round, ModRem, Negate, Invert, Hypot, Abs, Sign nodes) and implements `simplify_and_sort` (per CSS Values 4 simplification rules) and `resolve_internal` (the actual evaluation logic).\n\n## Overflow Handling (2): Numeric Overflow During calc() Resolution\n\nThe overflow/extreme-value handling happens at multiple layers:\n\n### Rust layer:\n- **`servo/components/style/values/generics/calc.rs`** (`resolve_internal`): Handles IEEE 754 special values – NaN propagation for operations like round(infinity, infinity), and infinity passthrough for round(infinity, finite). The result after resolution is a float that may be infinity or NaN.\n\n- **`servo/components/style/values/computed/length_percentage.rs`** (`CalcLengthPercentage::resolve`): After resolving the calc tree, it applies `clamping_mode.clamp(px)` (using `AllowedNumericType` e.g. NonNegative) and `.normalized()` to handle the float result before returning a `Length`.\n\n### Rust-to-C++ bridge:\n- **`servo/ports/geckolib/glue.rs`** (`Servo_ResolveCalcLengthPercentage`): Exported C-callable function that calls the Rust `resolve` and returns the result as a raw `f32`.\n\n### C++ layer:\n- **`layout/style/ServoStyleConstsInlines.h`** (`StyleCalcLengthPercentage::Resolve`, `LengthPercentage::Resolve`, `detail::DefaultPercentLengthToAppUnits`): Converts the resolved CSS-pixel float to `nscoord` (int32_t app units) using `NSToCoordTruncClamped`. The `DefaultPercentLengthToAppUnits` function wraps the conversion with bounds-checking.\n\n- **`gfx/src/nsCoord.h`** (`NSToCoordRoundWithClamp`, `NSToCoordTruncClamped`): The core C++ overflow protection. Both functions explicitly bounds-check the float value against `nscoord_MAX` and `nscoord_MIN` before converting to `int32_t`, preventing overflow crashes when calc() produces extreme values like very large pixel lengths."
}

  "text": "Firefox's CSS calc() implementation spans both Rust (Servo) and C++ layers.\n\n## (1) CSS calc() Expression Parsing\n\nThe primary parsing code lives in the Rust Servo style engine:\n\n- **`servo/components/style/values/specified/calc.rs`**: Defines the `MathFunction` enum (calc, min, max, clamp, round, mod, rem, sin, cos, tan, asin, acos, atan, atan2, pow, sqrt, hypot, log, exp, abs, sign), the `Leaf` enum for leaf node types (Length, Angle, Time, Resolution, Percentage, Number, ColorComponent), and the `CalcLengthPercentage` struct. `AllowParse` controls what units and additional functions (like anchor()) are permitted during parsing.\n\n- **`servo/components/style/values/generics/calc.rs`**: Defines `GenericCalcNode<L>` (the calc expression AST with Sum, Product, MinMax, Clamp, Round, ModRem, Negate, Invert, Hypot, Abs, Sign, Anchor, AnchorSize nodes). # Firefox CSS calc() Value Overflow

## Your Task

Firefox crashes when processing CSS calc() with extreme values. Find the C++ source files in mozilla/gecko-dev that (1) parse CSS calc() expressions and (2) handle numeric overflow during calc() value resolution.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the incident domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/firefox--871325b8.


## Output Format


- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

Let me search for crash tests related to CSS calc():
Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me write the answer:

The answer has been written to `/workspace/answer.json`. Here's a summary of my findings:

## CSS calc() in Firefox — Key Files

### Parsing (1)
- **`servo/components/style/values/specified/calc.rs`** — The main Rust parser for CSS `calc()` and related math functions (`min()`, `max()`, `clamp()`, `round()`, `sin()`, etc.). Defines the `MathFunction` enum, `Leaf` node types, and `CalcLengthPercentage`.
- **`servo/components/style/values/generics/calc.rs`** — Generic calc AST (`GenericCalcNode`) with parsing helpers and the `simplify_and_sort` implementation per CSS Values 4 spec.

### Overflow Handling (2)
The overflow protection forms a chain from Rust → C++ bridge → C++:

1. **`servo/components/style/values/generics/calc.rs`** (`resolve_internal`) — Handles IEEE 754 infinity/NaN in the Rust evaluation layer
2. **`servo/components/style/values/computed/length_percentage.rs`** (`resolve`) — Applies `clamping_mode` (NonNegative, etc.) after resolution
3. **`servo/ports/geckolib/glue.rs`** (`Servo_ResolveCalcLengthPercentage`) — C-callable bridge returning the resolved `f32`
4. **`layout/style/ServoStyleConstsInlines.h`** (`StyleCalcLengthPercentage::Resolve`, `DefaultPercentLengthToAppUnits`) — Converts CSS pixels to `nscoord` using clamped functions
5. **`gfx/src/nsCoord.h`** (`NSToCoordRoundWithClamp`, `NSToCoordTruncClamped`) — The key overflow protection: bounds-checks float values against `nscoord_MAX`/`nscoord_MIN` before converting to `int32_t`

65: impl ModRemOp {
66:     fn apply(self, dividend: f32, divisor: f32) -> f32 {
67:         // In mod(A, B) only, if B is infinite and A has opposite sign to B
68:         // (including an oppositely-signed zero), the result is NaN.
69:         // https://drafts.csswg.org/css-values/#round-infinities
70:         if matches!(self, Self::Mod)
71:             && divisor.is_infinite()
72:             && dividend.is_sign_negative() != divisor.is_sign_negative()
73:         {
74:             return f32::NAN;
75:         }
76: 
77:         let (r, same_sign_as) = match self {
78:             Self::Mod => (dividend - divisor * (dividend / divisor).floor(), divisor),
79:             Self::Rem => (dividend - divisor * (dividend / divisor).trunc(), dividend),
80:         };

  "text": "Firefox's CSS calc() implementation spans both Rust (Servo) and C++ layers.\n\n## Parsing (1): CSS calc() Expression Parsing\n\nThe primary parsing code lives in the Rust Servo style engine:\n\n- **`servo/components/style/values/specified/calc.rs`**: Defines the `MathFunction` enum (calc, min, max, clamp, round, mod, rem, sin, cos, tan, etc.), the `Leaf` enum for leaf node types (Length, Angle, Time, Resolution, Percentage, Number), and the `CalcLengthPercentage` struct. It also defines `AllowParse` which controls what units and additional functions (like anchor()) are permitted when parsing.\n\n- **`servo/components/style/values/generics/calc.rs`**: Defines `GenericCalcNode<L>` (the calc expression AST with Sum, Product, MinMax, Clamp, Round, ModRem, Negate, Invert, Hypot, Abs, Sign nodes) and implements `simplify_and_sort` (per CSS Values 4 simplification rules) and `resolve_internal` (the actual evaluation logic).\n\n## Overflow Handling (2): Numeric Overflow During calc() Resolution\n\nThe overflow/extreme-value handling happens at multiple layers:\n\n### Rust layer:\n- **`servo/components/style/values/generics/calc.rs`** (`resolve_internal`): Handles IEEE 754 special values – NaN propagation for operations like round(infinity, infinity), and infinity passthrough for round(infinity, finite). The result after resolution is a float that may be infinity or NaN.\n\n- **`servo/components/style/values/computed/length_percentage.rs`** (`CalcLengthPercentage::resolve`): After resolving the calc tree, it applies `clamping_mode.clamp(px)` (using `AllowedNumericType` e.g. NonNegative) and `.normalized()` to handle the float result before returning a `Length`.\n\n### Rust-to-C++ bridge:\n- **`servo/ports/geckolib/glue.rs`** (`Servo_ResolveCalcLengthPercentage`): Exported C-callable function that calls the Rust `resolve` and returns the result as a raw `f32`.\n\n### C++ layer:\n- **`layout/style/ServoStyleConstsInlines.h`** (`StyleCalcLengthPercentage::Resolve`, `LengthPercentage::Resolve`, `detail::DefaultPercentLengthToAppUnits`): Converts the resolved CSS-pixel float to `nscoord` (int32_t app units) using `NSToCoordTruncClamped`. The `DefaultPercentLengthToAppUnits` function wraps the conversion with bounds-checking.\n\n- **`gfx/src/nsCoord.h`** (`NSToCoordRoundWithClamp`, `NSToCoordTruncClamped`): The core C++ overflow protection. Both functions explicitly bounds-check the float value against `nscoord_MAX` and `nscoord_MIN` before converting to `int32_t`, preventing overflow crashes when calc() produces extreme values like very large pixel lengths."
}
